title: SR 13-21: Inspection Frequency and Scope Expectations for Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies that are Community Banking Organizations -- Revised December 16, 2022
title_normalized: Inspection Frequency and Scope Expectations for Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies that are Community Banking Organizations -- Revised December 16, 2022
section_path: Federal Reserve System > Supervision and Regulation > SR Letters > SR 13-21
issuing_agency: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
applicability_raw: to holding companies supervised by the Federal Reserve with less than $10 billion in total consolidated assets. Previously, this letter applied to the supervision of holding companies with $10 billion or less in total consolidated assets. This modification was made to better align with the supervisory portfolio definitions described on the Board’s public website.
section_headings: TO THE OFFICER IN CHARGE OF SUPERVISION AT EACH FEDERAL RESERVE BANK AND TO HOLDING COMPANIES WITH LESS THAN $10 BILLION IN TOTAL CONSOLIDATED ASSETS
bm25_text: SR 13-21 Inspection Frequency and Scope Expectations for Bank Holding Companies and Savings and Loan Holding Companies that are Community Banking Organizations -- Revised December 16, 2022 to holding companies supervised by the Federal Reserve with less than $10 billion in total consolidated assets. Previously, this letter applied to the supervision of holding companies with $10 billion or less in total consolidated assets. This modification was made to better align with the supervisory portfolio definitions described on the Board’s public website.
